title: boesenbergia plicata var. plicata
intro: Boesenbergia plicata var. plicata, commonly known as the tailed ginger or lesser galangal, is a plant species belonging to the family Zingiberaceae. It is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Thai and Indonesian herbal medicine, due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Boesenbergia plicata var. plicata are attributed to its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems, such as coughs and colds. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as in traditional remedies and tonics.
